label: Neon Postgres
summary: Use when a Next.js app needs server-side access to a hosted Neon Postgres database through the Neon serverless driver. Provides a lazy shared SQL client helper and a DB connectivity health route; when DATABASE_URL is missing the app must fall back to static seedData with a discreet config notice instead of crashing. Pair with separate migration or ORM tooling when schema management is needed.

envVars.purpose: Neon Postgres connection string used by the server-side database helper. Get it from the Neon project dashboard, preferably the pooled/serverless connection string with SSL enabled. Tagged feature-runtime because the dossier degrades gracefully without it: isDbConfigured() gates all access, pages render seedData + DbConfigNotice, and /api/health/db returns 503.

notes: Neon-specific alternative under capability 'database' (postgres-drizzle is default; this is picked on explicit Neon intent via relevanceKeywords). Legacy import wave 2: lazy getSql() (never module-level), health route re-rooted to @/lib/db and no longer returns raw DB errors, F2 seed-fallback contract added. lastVerified carries the legacy source's date until the first preview/generate-test selects this dossier (no-false-green).
